title: Enforce candidate materialization and post-merge source verification
rawUserRequest: 如果再遇到问题，先确认是不是处理流程有问题。如果是，先修正流程，再尝试发布。目标是完成 v0.5.39 发布；用户批准方案 A，并授权 agent 对后续流程问题自行确认、判断和执行。

declaredIntent.summary: Restore candidate-tree materialization, make the candidate-to-merge transition executable, and keep publication blocked until the merged default-branch source passes exact-SHA preflight.

intent.problem: The v0.5.39 candidate PR passed before merge but the merged default branch failed release preflight because premerge finalization hashed the old origin/main commit (23cb9fc8...) rather than the candidate tree that became the merge tree (b13cc3d2...). A previous candidate-tree fix was later reverted together with its unit expectation, so PR-local verification could not detect the transition failure.
constraints: Do not create or move the v0.5.39 tag until the corrective Work Item is merged, closed, synchronized, and the merged default branch passes release preflight and required CI.

Keep sourceCommit and tagTarget bound to the uniquely resolved merged default-branch commit through provider-generated release-source.json evidence; do not attempt to commit a SHA into its own commit.
Use the clean candidate branch HEAD only to materialize sourceTree and archiveSha256 during premerge finalization.
Preserve fail-closed behavior for identity, tree, archive, lifecycle, and ownership mismatches.
Archive count 538 and subsequent warning-only growth must remain non-blocking under the configured warning policy.
Do not bypass check-ai-pr or create a metadata-only PR without an owning Work Item.
intent.rationale: Candidate content and merged commit identity are different concerns. The candidate HEAD supplies the content digest before merge; the hosted release boundary resolves the exact merged default-branch SHA and publishes that immutable identity as external release evidence after merge.

outOfScope: Creating or publishing the v0.5.39 tag or GitHub Release inside this corrective Work Item
Weakening exact-SHA, controlled-origin-ref, archive, source-tree, or Work Item ownership validation
Changing release.json, release-state.json, release-freeze.json, or release-digests.json values before the corrected lifecycle reaches its release-preparation stage

sources.reason: Current premerge finalizer resolves the controlled origin ref and hashes the stale default-branch commit.
Post-merge preflight resolves the exact source identity and regenerates the canonical source tree and archive.
The existing fresh detached regression passes in PR topology but fails after merge; it is the authoritative local reproduction.
The hosted boundary already resolves the unique default branch, checks out its exact SHA, emits release-source.json, and runs preflight before tag creation.
Current documentation incorrectly instructs premerge finalization to hash the resolved old default-branch identity instead of candidate content.
Release metadata and Work Item evidence are export-ignored, allowing candidate and clean merge commits to share canonical archive content.

problemStatement: Make the release process test and enforce the real candidate-commit to merge-commit state transition so PR-local success cannot conceal a merged-main archive mismatch.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Premerge finalization hashes the clean candidate HEAD while retaining the controlled default-branch identity reference.
A real base commit, candidate commit, no-ff merge commit, and detached post-merge checkout reproduce the lifecycle and accept unchanged canonical content.
Post-merge preflight rejects a merge/default-branch source whose included canonical content differs from the frozen candidate digest.
The hosted release boundary records exact sourceCommit, tagTarget, and metadataCommit outside the self-referential source commit and verifies them before tag mutation.
Archive growth at 538 or higher remains warning-only when enforcement.archiveGrowth is warning.

riskAssessment.reason: The change is narrow, but an incorrect candidate/merge boundary could publish a tag whose archive evidence does not match its source.

executionDecision.reason: The user approved scheme A and delegated subsequent evidence-based process decisions; scope, sources, scenarios, acceptance, and verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review the real Git topology regression rather than relying only on mocked commit names.
Do not allow the corrective PR itself to create or move v0.5.39.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Preserve the source-binding, no-publication, and lifecycle-ownership constraints through a long release correction.

acceptance: Premerge finalization computes sourceTree and archiveSha256 from the clean candidate Work Item branch HEAD, while sourceCommit, tagTarget, and metadataCommit remain controlled identity references for post-merge resolution.

A regression test creates a real candidate commit and no-ff merge commit, checks out the merge commit detached in a fresh repository, and proves post-merge preflight accepts unchanged canonical content.
The regression fails against the current implementation for the observed stale-base archive mismatch and passes after the minimal finalizer correction.
An included-content drift case remains fail-closed before tag or release mutation.
Documentation distinguishes candidate content materialization from merged default-branch identity and preserves the external release-source.json authority.

The superseded plan that prescribes hashing old origin/main is removed; transient design and execution-plan artifacts remain auditable in branch history but are removed from the final tree so markdown complexity does not grow.
Archive growth 538 remains warning-only and is covered by focused verification.
The corrective Work Item completes Contract, Preflight, tests, ai-finish/archive, check-ai-pr, PR, merge, ai-close-work-item, and default-branch synchronization before any v0.5.39 release attempt.
After closure, the exact merged main SHA passes smoke, compatibility, release preflight, and release evidence audit before v0.5.39 is tagged and published.
guidelines: Fail closed on release identity, lifecycle, source-tree, archive, or ownership mismatch.
Keep candidate content evidence distinct from provider-bound published identity evidence.
Use one Work Item, one branch, and one PR for the corrective process change.
Do not rewrite immutable archived Work Item evidence.
Do not publish or tag until post-merge verification is complete.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user approved scheme A and delegated future evidence-based release-process decisions to the agent.

rollbackNote: Revert the finalizer, regression, and documentation changes together; do not retain a test expectation that permits hashing stale origin/main content.

budgetImpact.reason: One functional corrective Work Item archive plus bounded regression coverage; archive growth remains warning-only.
